feedback.text: I think it's very important to realize that the "digital market" is rapidly becoming just "the market" for many things... Whatever rules are set here will apply to anything that has any electronics in it, or is in any way expected to interact with anything containing electronics. Digital additions are used to circumvent centuries of consumer rights. Even buying a dishwasher can mean you're expected to create an account with the manufacturer (example: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=5M_hmwBBPnc ) Seeing the recent political landscape in for example the USA, what will Europe do when Elon Musk holds all Teslas in the EU hostage during some negotiations? It's unlikely to happen, but it's perfectly possible. If Tesla decides a specific car shouldn't be usable, it won't be, and the owner is powerless against it. Everything requires accounts to be used (televisions, dishwashers, cameras, printers, cars, thermostats, baby monitors, computer programs, ....), and the consumer is completely powerless against how this has changed all conventions that used to be there when buying something. The terms of the purchase can and are constantly changed after the sale. you'll receive the usual e-mail "we're updating our terms of service, click here to 'agree'". Not agreeing of course means you can no longer use your car/dishwasher/camera/software/baby monitor/.... This act is called the Digital Fairness Act, but look around you right now, how many of the important things in your life are "digital" (electronic in any way). Anything using any kinds of electronics is digital, and this act will apply to it. Looking at the summary, it's thinking of websites, addictive games/gambling, things happening on social media, .... But soon your car, dishwasher, washing machine, mobile phone, thermostat, baby monitor, computer, stereo, watch, (security) camera, printer, medical devices, 3d printer, lights, wall switches, kitchen appliances, sewing machine, ... will have some digital aspect to them, and will follow the same practices we're talking about here. Please realize that whatever rules you set here will apply to the next car you buy, how you'll be able to use your dishwasher, etc... Ideally a part of digital fairness will be "accounts can't be required for functionality of devices". and also "The terms of usage are agreed on when the sale is made, and not altered afterwards". Anything in this act that doesn't make the digital world act exactly the same as the non digital world, will mean that many very important things in our modern world will use digital means to circumvent all the rights we would have if things were not digital. You could just repair your dishwasher yourself in the past? Or get any repairman to have a look? Too bad, it's digital now, parts require an activation from the manufacturer, only an official repairman with official parts is allowed to service it. Seems silly for a dishwasher, but for IPhones this is already reality, and thinking practices like this won't expand to other devices is naive... So please consider that this digital fairness act, could just as well be just called the "fairness act", since it'll determine what is fair for a whole lot of important items in our society, not just some websites & social media. Your car, appliances, medical devices, gadgets, office devices, home electronics, .... will all use whatever is in here as a template of what they can do, and not whatever rules there are for physical goods. Because even if the smallest part of them is digital, processing power is dirt cheap, and making them dependent on some central service and using digital limitations to determine how you can use them. Not the old paradigm of 'you own it, you use it however you want'.
